title: Child Guidance * At Risk Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Child Guidance * At Risk Families 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues At Risk Families Child/Adolescent Sex Offenders Mental Health Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER DOUGLAS COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family support programs, in-home support and counseling, crisis intervention, parent education classes and support groups, community resource and referrals, and play therapy for children. Offers classes for young children with trained teachers. 300 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 673-4354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PIONEER RELIEF NURSERY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides family support, referrals, advocacy, respite care, and parenting classes to parents who are experiencing high levels of stress. Therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers and home visits provided as well. 1312 SW 2nd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Parents experiencing stress with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 215-1017 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY EAST PORTLAND FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting education, support through home visits, and group activities and intensive case management. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 2609 SE 136th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: High risk families with children ages 6 weeks to 5 years old. Intakes are completed to determine eligibility. Hours: Therapeutic Early Childhood Program: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-12:30pm Parent Education Classes: Offered quarterly one afternoon a week Respite Care: Monday and Wednesday afternoon for home based and waitlisted families Email (503) 972-9643 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER DOUGLAS COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY PIONEER RELIEF NURSERY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY SERVICES VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY
